issues.title: TextField fires onWindowShown event inappropriately on  Android platform.
issues.body:
## TextField calls onWindowShown of IME inappropriately.

The following video shows an example where onWindowShown is called every time text is entered, causing input problems.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/58111771/120742708-621c7180-c532-11eb-8b3d-80cd3b2dea44.mov

I tried` flutter doctor -v`

```
[✓] Flutter (Channel stable, 2.2.1, on macOS 11.2.3 20D91 darwin-x64, locale ja-JP)
    • Flutter version 2.2.1 at /Users/chiakikajiwara/develop/flutter
    • Framework revision 02c026b03c (7 days ago), 2021-05-27 12:24:44 -0700
    • Engine revision 0fdb562ac8
    • Dart version 2.13.1
```
This behavior occurs in Flutter version >= 2.2.0.
And I have confirmed that this does not occur on iOS platform.
Thank you for your cooperation.
comments:
Hi @chiaki-kjwr 
Thanks for filing the issue.
Does the issue reproduce with an English keyboard too? 
Can you please provide a complete reproducible minimal code sample, and complete `flutter run --verbose` logs running the minimal code sample?
Thank you

Thank you for your reply.
I will answer the question from you in the following way.

> Does the issue reproduce with an English keyboard too?

Yes.I Confirmed that the problem is reproduced.

> complete reproducible minimal code sample

An exapmle follows.(This is a part of my flutter sample app)

```
void main() {
  runApp(MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      theme: ThemeData(
        primarySwatch: Colors.blue,
      ),
      home: MyHomePage(title: 'Flutter Demo Home Page'),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  MyHomePage({Key key, this.title}) : super(key: key);
final String title;

  @override
  _MyHomePageState createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
@override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {

 return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(
        title: Text(widget.title),
      ),
      body: Center(
        child: Column(
               mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
               children: <Widget>[
  Padding(
              padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(vertical: 8),
              child: TextField(
                autofocus: true,
                minLines: 1,
                maxLines: 20,
                maxLength: 100,
                style: Theme.of(context).textTheme.headline4,
 ),
            ),
          ],
        ),
      ),
}
```

complete logs follow.

@TahaTesser 

Thank you for your reply.

I tried several IMEs and found out that the problem depends on an IME.
However, Checking the log, it seems that` showSoftInput()` is called unnecessarily.

```
D/InputMethodManager( 2905): showSoftInput() view=io.flutter.embedding.android.FlutterView{45bbbba VFE...... .F...... 0,0-1080,2148 aid=1073741824} flags=0
D/InsetsController( 2905): show(ime(), fromIme=true)

```
In Flutter 2.0.6, `showSoftInput()` is only called at once when a software keyboard appears.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/58111771/121155028-6920e800-c882-11eb-8dd6-e239cb3fe81e.mov

On the other hand, in flutter 2.2.1, `showSoftInput() `is called per text entering.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/58111771/121155126-7ccc4e80-c882-11eb-8020-d9f792f6135e.mov

My environment:
```
API Version: 30
OS: Android 11
```
